title: Charred Tender British Rum Eton-Mess
description: A diabetic-friendly twist on the classic Eton-Mess dessert, featuring charred tender British rum-soaked strawberries and a light dashi extract glaze.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
In a small saucepan, heat the rum over medium heat until it starts to simmer.
Add the brown sugar to the saucepan and stir until dissolved.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
In a separate saucepan, whisk together the dashi extract, almond extract (if using), and coconut milk.
Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat.
Reduce the heat to low and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the mixture has thickened slightly.
In a separate bowl, whip the heavy cream until stiff peaks form.
Add the beaten egg whites to the bowl and whip until stiff peaks form.
Melt the dark chocolate chips in a double boiler or in the microwave in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval.
In a large bowl, combine the whipped cream and egg whites mixture, the melted chocolate, and the coconut milk mixture.
Fold the mixture until well combined.
In a separate bowl, mix together the granulated sugar and lemon juice.
Add the mixture to the bowl with the cream mixture and fold until well combined.
To assemble the Eton-Mess, place a layer of the cream mixture in the bottom of a serving dish.
Top the cream mixture with a layer of sliced strawberries, then drizzle with the rum syrup.
Repeat the layers two more times, finishing with a layer of cream mixture on top.
Garnish with chopped fresh mint leaves and serve immediately.
To char the strawberries, heat a grill pan or broiler to medium-high heat.
Place the sliced strawberries on the grill pan or under the broiler and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, or until slightly charred.
